draft.introduction: The “Great Hallel,” a litany with the refrain “for his steadfast love endures forever” repeated in every verse. Used in Jewish Passover liturgy and adopted into Christian worship.

translation.text: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever. Give thanks to the God of gods, for his steadfast love endures forever. To him who alone does great wonders, to him who made the heavens with understanding.
translation.original_text: ἐξομολογεῖσθε τῷ κυρίῳ ὅτι ἀγαθός ὅτι εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τὸ ἔλεος αὐτοῦ

translator_notes.note: The Greek ἔλεος (“mercy, steadfast love”) translates Hebrew hesed, one of the most theologically loaded terms in the OT, denoting covenantal faithfulness that goes beyond legal obligation.
